title: Sieberia albida
intro: Sieberia albida, also known as Sieber's beech fern, is a species of fern native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Sieberia albida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Sieberia albida are often available in capsule or tea form, and may also be incorporated into topical creams and ointments for local application.
